exchanges.question: You're an AI engineer by profession building a production company on the side. That's two very different worlds - one pays the bills, one feeds the soul. How do you think about work? Is your engineering job something you tolerate to fund the dream, or do you genuinely find meaning in both? What's your relationship with work itself?
exchanges.response: It all started when I thought nothing should be a black box for me to understand. In my engineering, everything was hyped up related to AI and I thought I don't wanna be in that gorrela mandha, I want to know everything, I now know everything, You have a solid basics background and any new development will be easily understood. Naku cooking ante passion, oka kitchen model tayaru chesi, every kitchen lo install chesi, after that it teaches you how to cook, it looks at you always, it's like a proper guidance at every step, you can learn how to cook. Create new recipes. Have a global level competition with it. My final aim is to make every kitchen in the world. Cook a same meal as the meal of the day of the day and I handle the supply chain of the products too. Then later, I was also interested in a long-term goal but I couldn't lose passion, I started building this AI script writing model and also added a kitchen model under orchestrator. Atleast nenu use chesukovocchu ani, if success aythe peddha companies ki ammocchu ani.

exchanges.question: 'Atleast nenu use chesukovocchu, if success aythe peddha companies ki ammocchu' - that's a practical mindset. Build for yourself first, monetize later. But you're building THREE things now - Friday (script AI), kitchen model, and production company. How do you decide where to put your energy on any given day? Do you have a system, or does it depend on mood and momentum?
exchanges.response: It depends on my modern momentum. I started with the script model and I'm currently working on it. Okkadine kurchoni chesthunna kabatti, It'll take time. One step at a time.

exchanges.question: 'Okkadine kurchoni chesthunna' - working alone on all of this. Solo building is hard because there's no one to push you on bad days and no one to celebrate with on good days. When you hit a wall - the code isn't working, the story isn't clicking, you're just stuck - what do you do? Do you push through, step away, or have some ritual that gets you unstuck?
exchanges.response: Take a deep breath, clear you mind, do meditation, listen to songs, go to gym. These will be my basic things to do, And also telugu song lyrics contains immense depth and romance where you can easily get inspired to create a scene, Ippudu unna scenes evi kuda aa song lyrics ki justify cheyyaledhu, idhi na opinion.
telugu_words_detected: Ippudu unna scenes evi kuda aa song lyrics ki justify cheyyaledhu

exchanges.question: That's a sharp observation - 'Ippudu unna scenes evi kuda aa song lyrics ki justify cheyyaledhu.' The lyrics carry more weight than the visuals. Who writes lyrics that actually move you? Which lyricist or composer do you go back to when you need that depth - whether for inspiration or just to feel something?
exchanges.response: Sirivennela seetharama sastry, sri sri, veturi sundararama murthy, Ramajogayya sastry, Anantha sreeram, Chandrabose, suddhala ashok teja.

exchanges.question: Sirivennela's philosophical depth, Veturi's romantic precision, Sri Sri's revolutionary fire - all very different energies. When you're writing for your production company, which of these styles do you naturally lean toward? Or do you see yourself developing a voice that's different from all of them?
exchanges.response: I lean towards Sirivennela seetharama sastry.

exchanges.question: Let's talk about the other side - failure. You're building Friday, planning a production company, working a day job. If three years from now, none of this worked - Friday didn't pan out, the production company never launched, you're still just doing your engineering job - how would you handle that? Would it break you, or is there a backup mindset you carry?
exchanges.response: I am always saying life is not that serious, I'll keep on trying. Vadhile prasakthe ledhu. I have clarity on one thing. icchina jeevithanni waste cheyyakudadhu, nacchinattu brathaki, At the end od the manishiki kavalsindhi mental peace, proper food. That's it.

exchanges.question: 'Mental peace, proper food' - you've reduced life to its essentials. You mentioned earlier you're building Friday to help with scripts, but you're also the one training it. When you imagine the ideal work session - you, Friday, working on a screenplay - what does that look like? What would Friday need to do to make you feel like you're not working alone anymore?
exchanges.response: Give me different perspectives where I lack, Help me with the story building. Connect with the external world to gather data and provide answers my way. Na thought okate, ippudu nenu oka book chadivina manishi tho kurchunte script ela rasthanu? , oka 10 books chadivina manishi tho kurchunte ela rasthanu? , adhe unlimited data, books telisina vaaditho kurchunte ela rasthanu? If my model have the knowledge of unlimited books and have the access to internet articles, every other data, if I write with it to complete my script, that's how I feel satisfied output. I feel it like a masterpiece.
telugu_words_detected: ippudu nenu oka book chadivina manishi tho kurchunte script ela rasthanu
oka 10 books chadivina manishi tho kurchunte ela rasthanu
books telisina vaaditho kurchunte ela rasthanu

exchanges.question: Last one for work - you've talked about Friday, the production company, the kitchen model. You've mentioned competing with Warner Bros. You want to stay active for 50 years. When you're 70-80, looking back at all of this, what would make you say 'I used my time well'? What's the version of success that matters to you beyond money and fame?
exchanges.response: I want to use my given free will to make myself happy mentally and healthy. I don't want to regret any day, not living the life to the fullest. It's not the money or fame that you need, it's about what and which quality Circle are you living with, I want to build a quality network around the world with whom I'll be making decisions together, making moments, helping out for one another.
